invariants.description: Non-portable domain paths are relative, traversal-free, and never absolute.
Identity roles and scopes are unique and administrative audiences carry the administrative role.
Identity tenant, audience, scopes, and idempotency satisfy the requested operation.
Local authorities have no endpoint and cloud authorities require one HTTPS endpoint.
Authority capability identifiers are unique.
Authority handshakes bind exact current digests and the sorted capability inventory.
Task tags are unique and completed tasks carry a completion timestamp.
Task status transitions follow the closed lifecycle and terminal states do not reopen.
Agent role identifiers are unique.
A dependency cannot point a task at itself.
Git object identifiers have the exact hexadecimal length required by their algorithm.
Every page count equals the exact number of returned items.
Operation identifiers and surfaces are unique, derived, mode-correct, and semantically complete.
Task update requests contain at least one changed field.
Transfer execution checkpoints bind the source, target, bundle, plan, and canonical digests.
Operation invocations bind exact current contract and manifest digests.
Invocation authority equals the validated organization and tenant identity.
The operation, mode, scopes, idempotency, and typed request all match the manifest.
The descriptor closes over current manifest, capability, schema, invariant, provenance, and generator digests.
Every section, record, nested reference, projection, closure, attachment, and inventory entry has one source authority.
Public transfer validation binds exact current contract and manifest digests.
Public import execution binds exact current contract and manifest digests.
Every section count and digest and the bundle checksum match canonical content.
Portable records exclude raw commands, arguments, paths, credentials, and execution internals.
Every portable record participates in a complete transitive reference closure; projection predecessors resolve by exact owner, kind, id, version, and digest.
Task dependency closure is complete, deterministic, and acyclic.
Evidence, command output, file, and artifact payloads are represented only by SHA-256 content references.
Deletion history contains digest-only full-redaction tombstones and no raw payload.
Import plans carry a deterministic id plus a content digest binding source and target authorities, canonical digests, bundle content, conflicts, and counts.
Checkpoints bind source, target, bundle id and digest, import-plan id and digest, contract and manifest digests, and idempotency.
Checkpoint progress advances one canonical section at a time to one terminal state.
Execution requests and optional checkpoints bind every source, target, digest, plan, bundle, and idempotency field.
Execution context is exactly uncommitted or committed with one valid receipt; all unknown states fail closed.
Receipts bind source, target, bundle id and digest, import-plan id and digest, contract and manifest digests, counts, and one terminal checkpoint.
Migration receipts form one strict digest-linked chain where each idempotency key has one canonical import tuple and terminal result; exact receipt replay never appends.

Every public checkpoint, execution, receipt, transition, receipt-chain, and operation-map boundary rejects historical contract or manifest digests; version-neutral foundation, registry, and generated schemas remain internal structural inputs.
Only an identical committed import replays; conflicts and unknown context reject.
Projection references are opaque identifiers, never paths or URLs.
All identity, pull-request, proof, and predecessor refs match the projection owner and required kind.
Published, provider-observed, and equality-proof heads are complete and equal to the branch head.
Proof references and digests are unique, owner-bound, kind-bound, and tied to the current head.
Projection digests cover canonical content and successors bind exact immediate predecessors.
Full histories reject missing links, ABA heads, repeats, substitutions, owner or kind drift, and stale heads.
Checked-in artifacts match canonical regenerated bytes even when checksums are internally recomputed.
